sections.paragraph:
Μεσσίας, ου, ὁ Hellenized transliteration of מָשִׁיחַ, Aram. מְשִׁיחָא (s. Schürer II 517, and n. 16 w. ref. to Dalman, Gramm.2 157, 3) the Messiah = the Anointed One (ThNöldeke, ZDMG 32, 1878, 403; W-S. §5, 26c p. 57 note 54) in our lit. only twice, and in J: in the mouth of a disciple J 1:41 and of a Samaritan woman 4:25, in both cases translated by Χριστός, q.v.—BHHW II 1197–1204; ABD IV 777–88; Schürer II 488–54; HKippenberg, Garizim u. Synagoge ’71, on Messianic expectation 115f; JCharlesworth, ed., The Messiah, Developments in Earliest Judaism and Christianity ’92; IGrünewald et al., edd., Messiah and Christos, DFlusser Festschr. ’92.—M-M. TW.

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

ZDMG ZDMG = Zeitschrift der Deutschen Morgenländischen Gesellschaft—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

ABD ABD = Anchor Bible Dictionary—List 6

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

edd. edd. = editions, editors

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 635
